caption: Gen Z protesters vandalizing and attacking Pashupatinath Temple in Kathmandu September 2025
full_claim: Indian TV channels NDTV and Times Now Navbharat shared footage claiming Gen Z protesters had attacked the sacred Pashupatinath Temple during Nepal's September 2025 uprising.
true_context: BOOM fact-checked the video and conclusively traced it to the Basleshwar Jatra Festival held at Pashupatinath in March 2025 — six months before the Gen Z protests. No protesters attacked the temple. The footage was from a completely different religious event.
article_title: No, Gen Z Protesters Did Not Attack Pashupatinath Temple — Video Is From Basleshwar Jatra March 2025
named_entities: NDTV, Times Now, Pashupatinath Temple, Gen Z Protest, Basleshwar Jatra, Kathmandu

caption: Nepal Gen Z protesters carrying Yogi Adityanath photo during September 2025 uprising
full_claim: A post on X claimed that protesters in Nepal's Gen Z uprising had carried Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath's photo during the September 2025 demonstrations.
true_context: BOOM traced the image to a pro-monarchy rally in Kathmandu in March 2025 — six months before the Gen Z protests began. The image had absolutely no connection to the Gen Z movement against the Oli government.
article_title: Photo Showing Yogi Adityanath's Picture at Nepal Protests Is From Pro-Monarchy Rally in March 2025
named_entities: Yogi Adityanath, Gen Z Protest, Pro-Monarchy Rally, Kathmandu

caption: Indian media broadcasting false news about Nepal's Gen Z protests
full_claim: Multiple Indian news channels including News18, IBC24, NDTV, and Times Now Navbharat broadcast false claims about Nepal's Gen Z protests, including: Oli left a secret resignation letter blaming Lord Ram controversy; protesters attacked Pashupatinath; businessman Durga Parsai orchestrated the protests.
true_context: BOOM fact-checked all major claims: No secret letter about Ram existed — Oli's letter cited constitutional procedures. Pashupatinath video was from a March 2025 festival. Protestors publicly denied Parsai's involvement. Indian channels fabricated a 'Hindu nation' narrative not supported by facts.
article_title: How Indian Media's Misinformation Machine Broke Its Ties With Nepal During Gen Z Protests
named_entities: News18, IBC24, NDTV, Times Now, KP Sharma Oli, Durga Parsai, Gen Z Protest, Nepal

caption: KP Sharma Oli's secret resignation letter blaming opposition to Lord Ram in Ayodhya for his ouster
full_claim: Indian news channels News18 and IBC24 claimed former Prime Minister KP Sharma Oli had left behind a secret resignation letter blaming his ouster on opposition to Lord Ram's birthplace in Ayodhya, framing it as a Hindu-Muslim political issue.
true_context: BOOM confirmed no such letter existed. Oli's actual resignation letter, publicly available, cited 'extraordinary circumstances' and constitutional procedures under Article 77(1)(a). There was no mention of Lord Ram, Ayodhya, or any religious controversy. The Indian channels fabricated the religious angle entirely.
article_title: False: Oli Did Not Leave Secret Resignation Letter Blaming Opposition to Lord Ram's Birthplace
named_entities: KP Sharma Oli, News18, IBC24, Lord Ram, Ayodhya, Gen Z Protest

caption: KP Sharma Oli is the only Nepali prime minister who never visited India
full_claim: Viral social media claims and some news reports stated that KP Sharma Oli was the only Nepali prime minister who never visited India during his tenure.
true_context: BOOM and Alt News confirmed the claim was false. Oli made official state visits to India in 2016 and 2018 during his previous terms as PM. The claim was either misinformed or deliberately false.
article_title: False: KP Sharma Oli Was Not the Only Nepali PM Never to Visit India — He Visited in 2016 and 2018
named_entities: KP Sharma Oli, India, Narendra Modi

caption: Earthquake destroying buildings in Nepal during Gen Z protests September 2025
full_claim: AI-generated images showing earthquake devastation were shared during Nepal's Gen Z protests with claims that an earthquake had struck Nepal simultaneously with the political crisis.
true_context: BOOM identified the images as AI-generated through visual analysis and detector tools. No earthquake struck Nepal during the September 2025 protests. The images were created to amplify the chaos narrative during the political uprising.
article_title: AI-Generated Earthquake Images Viral During Nepal Gen Z Protests With False Claims

caption: India's new Parliament building Akhand Bharat mural depicting Nepal as part of ancient India
full_claim: Nepal's political leaders and social media users claimed that India's new Parliament building unveiled a mural of 'Akhand Bharat' (Greater India) that depicted Nepal's territory as historically part of India.
true_context: BOOM verified the claim as accurate. The mural in India's new Parliament building did depict an 'Akhand Bharat' map showing an ancient South Asian civilization that encompassed present-day Nepal. Nepal formally protested through its Foreign Ministry.
article_title: India's New Parliament Akhand Bharat Mural Shows Nepal Territory — Claim Verified
named_entities: India, Nepal, Akhand Bharat, Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Narendra Modi

caption: Nepal's interim Prime Minister Sushila Karki is a CIA agent working for India and USA
full_claim: After Sushila Karki was appointed as Nepal's interim Prime Minister, viral posts on TikTok and Facebook claimed she was a 'CIA agent' and 'India's puppet' installed to serve foreign interests.
true_context: No credible evidence supported these claims. Karki was a former Chief Justice of Nepal's Supreme Court with a distinguished judicial career. BOOM found no credible source linking her to the CIA or any foreign government as an agent.
article_title: False: Viral Posts Calling Nepal's PM Sushila Karki a 'CIA Agent' and 'India's Puppet' Are Baseless
named_entities: Sushila Karki, CIA, India, Nepal Prime Minister, Supreme Court

caption: 10 million Nepalis signed petition to restore Gyanendra Shah as king of Nepal
full_claim: Social media posts claimed over 10 million Nepalis had signed a petition demanding the restoration of Gyanendra Shah as king of Nepal, making it one of the most popular petitions in South Asian history.
true_context: BOOM verified the petition existed but had far fewer signatures — well under 500,000 at peak. Many signatures appeared from outside Nepal or were duplicates. The '10 million' figure was greatly exaggerated and the petition was not verified as authentic.
article_title: Viral Claim of Millions Signing Petition to Restore King Gyanendra Is Exaggerated
named_entities: Gyanendra Shah, Rastriya Prajatantra Party, Nepal Monarchy

caption: Viral deepfake and manipulated videos of Nepali political candidates during 2022 elections
full_claim: During Nepal's November 2022 general elections, multiple viral videos purported to show political candidates including KP Oli, Prachanda, and Rabi Lamichhane making controversial statements or performing embarrassing actions.
true_context: BOOM fact-checked multiple viral election videos and found they included manipulated audio, AI-generated voices, spliced clips from unrelated events, and Bollywood audio superimposed on politician videos. None of the most viral claims were authentic.
article_title: Nepal 2022 Elections: Manipulated Videos and Deepfakes Spread Misinformation About Candidates
named_entities: KP Sharma Oli, Pushpa Kamal Dahal, Rabi Lamichhane, Nepal Elections 2022

caption: Balen Shah announcing he has joined Rastriya Swatantra Party as its prime ministerial candidate
full_claim: A viral video was shared claiming to show Balen Shah making an early announcement that he was joining Rabi Lamichhane's Rastriya Swatantra Party and becoming its prime ministerial candidate.
true_context: The video was from a different event and the timeline of Balen's RSP joining was misrepresented. Balen formally joined RSP on December 28, 2025. Videos circulating before this date claiming an earlier joining were misleading.
article_title: Old Video Falsely Claims Balen Shah Announced Joining RSP Much Earlier Than He Did
named_entities: Balen Shah, RSP, Rastriya Swatantra Party, Rabi Lamichhane

caption: US MCC agreement overrides Nepal's constitution and threatens its sovereignty
full_claim: Viral posts, opposition politicians, and street protesters claimed the US Millennium Challenge Corporation (MCC) compact with Nepal would override Nepal's constitution, laws, and national sovereignty, making Nepal a vassal of the United States.
true_context: BOOM and legal experts reviewed the MCC compact and found it contains standard language used in all MCC agreements worldwide. It does not supersede Nepal's constitution. The 'sovereignty threat' narrative was politically motivated exaggeration from parties opposed to the government.
article_title: MCC Compact Does Not Override Nepal's Constitution or Sovereignty — Viral Claims Exaggerated
named_entities: MCC, United States, Nepal Government, Pushpa Kamal Dahal, KP Sharma Oli

caption: India's new political map 2019 includes Nepal's Kalapani territory
full_claim: Nepali politicians and social media posts claimed India's new political map released in November 2019 included Nepal's Kalapani, Lipulekh, and Limpiyadhura territories within Indian borders.
true_context: BOOM verified this claim as accurate. India's new political map did show these territories as part of India. Nepal's Ministry of Foreign Affairs issued a formal protest. This was a verified factual claim, not misinformation.
article_title: Verified: India's 2019 Political Map Did Include Nepal's Kalapani, Lipulekh and Limpiyadhura
named_entities: India, Nepal, Kalapani, Lipulekh, Limpiyadhura, Ministry of Foreign Affairs

caption: PM Prachanda and PM Modi in secret deal to hand over Nepal's water and hydropower to India
full_claim: Viral social media posts claimed PM Pushpa Kamal Dahal 'Prachanda' had entered into a secret agreement with Indian PM Narendra Modi to hand over Nepal's water resources and hydropower projects to India at below-market rates.
true_context: No credible documentary evidence of any such secret deal was found. While Nepal-India Power Trade Agreements existed for specific projects, these were publicly negotiated through the Nepal Electricity Authority. The 'secret deal' claim was fabricated without evidence.
article_title: Viral Claim of Secret Deal Between Prachanda and Modi to Divide Nepal's Resources Is False
named_entities: Pushpa Kamal Dahal, Prachanda, Narendra Modi, India, Nepal Electricity Authority

caption: Over 100 protesters killed by Nepal security forces during Gen Z uprising September 2025
full_claim: Multiple viral social media posts and some Indian news outlets claimed over 100 protesters were killed by security forces during Nepal's Gen Z uprising in September 2025.
true_context: BOOM fact-checked the figures against official government and hospital records. The official death toll from the September 8-9 protests was 19 deaths on the first day. While the total across subsequent days rose, claims of '100+ killed' were significantly exaggerated compared to verified figures.
article_title: Claims That Over 100 Were Killed in Nepal Gen Z Protests Are Exaggerated — Official Count Lower
named_entities: Gen Z Protest, Nepal Police, Kathmandu, Nepal Government

caption: Kathmandu Mayor Balen Shah displaying Greater Nepal map including Indian territory in his office
full_claim: Viral posts and news reports shared photos of Kathmandu Mayor Balen Shah displaying a 'Greater Nepal' map in his office that included territories currently within India, claiming it as expansionist and diplomatically reckless.
true_context: BOOM provided full context: Balen placed the Greater Nepal map in June 2023 as a direct response to India's Parliament unveiling an Akhand Bharat mural. The map was a political counter-statement, not an unprovoked territorial claim. Both the trigger (Akhand Bharat) and Balen's response were verified facts.
article_title: Balen Shah's Greater Nepal Map at KMC Office — Context Needed: It Was a Response to India's Akhand Bharat
named_entities: Balen Shah, KMC, Greater Nepal, Akhand Bharat, India, Kathmandu

caption: Balen Shah banning all Indian movies in Kathmandu because Bollywood claims Sita was born in India
full_claim: Viral posts claimed Kathmandu Mayor Balen Shah banned ALL Indian films in Kathmandu because a Bollywood movie had claimed Sita was born in India.
true_context: Balen did ban the screening of a specific Indian film in Kathmandu that made claims about Sita's birthplace conflicting with Nepal's position. However, viral posts exaggerated this to a blanket ban on 'all Indian films.' The ban was specific, not general, and was a political-cultural response to a particular film.
article_title: Balen Shah Banned Indian Films Claiming Sita Was Born in India — Context and Fact Check
named_entities: Balen Shah, Kathmandu Metropolitan City, Sita, India, Bollywood

caption: Lobbying underway to nominate former crown princess Himani Shah to National Assembly
full_claim: Social media and news reports claimed lobbying was underway to pick former crown princess Himani Shah as a lawmaker in the National Assembly based on her contribution to the social sector.
true_context: Khabarhub's fact check found no credible confirmation of such lobbying. The reports were based on unverified social media speculation and no official party or government source confirmed the nomination plan.
article_title: FACT CHECK: How Reports Say Lobby Underway to Nominate Himani Shah to Upper House

caption: 32 missing persons found dead inside Nepal's Parliament building
full_claim: Widespread social media claims stated that 32 bodies were discovered inside the Parliament building during the Gen Z protests.
true_context: Nepal Police refuted the claim via official Facebook statement, clarifying that violent images circulating online were taken from unrelated incidents abroad and being misused to spread panic. No bodies were found inside Parliament.
article_title: Nepal Police Denies Rumors of 32 Bodies Found Inside Parliament

caption: Air hostess video claiming KP Sharma Oli spotted fleeing to Dubai in a wheelchair
full_claim: A viral video showed an air hostess claiming Nepali Prime Minister KP Sharma Oli was spotted fleeing the country to an unknown destination via Dubai, being carried in a wheelchair by her colleague under the security of authorities.
true_context: Himalaya Airlines issued two official press notes denying the claim, stating it was 'completely false and misleading.' Oli had not fled the country. The video coincided with political tensions during Gen Z protests but was fabricated.
article_title: Viral Video of Air Hostess Claiming PM Oli Fleeing to Dubai Is False
named_entities: KP Sharma Oli, Himalaya Airlines, Dubai, Gen Z Protest

caption: Viral video claiming Nepali Congress general secretary Gagan Thapa being dragged and beaten by protesters
full_claim: A viral video showed a man being dragged and kicked by a mob, with claims identifying him as Nepali Congress general secretary Gagan Thapa during the Gen Z uprising.
true_context: The man in the video was not Gagan Thapa. The identity was misattributed. Thapa was not attacked during the protests. The video used unrelated footage with false identity claims superimposed.
article_title: Viral Video Falsely Claims Gagan Thapa Being Beaten by Mob During Gen Z Protests
named_entities: Gagan Thapa, Nepali Congress, Gen Z Protest, Kathmandu

caption: Deputy PM and Finance Minister Bishnu Prasad Paudel running in river to escape protesters
full_claim: A viral video claimed to show then Deputy Prime Minister and Finance Minister Bishnu Prasad Paudel running in a river to save himself from protesters who were pursuing and beating him during the Gen Z uprising.
true_context: The video did not show Bishnu Paudel. The identity was falsely attributed to the person in the video. Paudel was not attacked in the manner described during the protests.
article_title: Viral Video Does Not Show Deputy PM Bishnu Paudel Running in River from Protesters

caption: Nepal Army joining protesters against the government
full_claim: Multiple social media posts and viral videos claimed the Nepal Army had taken sides with protesters and was about to act against the Oli government during the Gen Z uprising.
true_context: Nepal Army issued a public notice urging citizens not to believe or spread false and misleading news reports linked to the military amid the political crisis. The Army maintained its constitutional neutrality and took no such action.
article_title: Nepal Army Tells Public Not to Believe False or Misleading News Linked to Military

caption: Sher Bahadur Deuba's son Jaybir Deuba bought international Hilton hotel chain using corrupt money
full_claim: YouTube influencer Dilbhushan Pathak and viral social media posts claimed that Jaybir Deuba, son of Nepali Congress president Sher Bahadur Deuba, had purchased the international hotel chain Hilton using corrupt political money.
true_context: The only fact substantiated was that a share purchase between the hotel owners and Jaybir was denied. Pathak's claims were based on assumptions and hearsay with no documentary evidence presented. The viral content misrepresented speculation as established fact.

caption: Nepal's six former prime ministers issuing joint retirement statement from politics
full_claim: Viral reports claimed Nepal's six former prime ministers — KP Sharma Oli, Sher Bahadur Deuba, Pushpa Kamal Dahal, Madhav Kumar Nepal, Jhalanath Khanal and Baburam Bhattarai — would issue a joint statement announcing their retirement from active politics.
true_context: While internal party pressure on older leaders was real following the Gen Z protests, no joint retirement statement was issued. Most leaders subsequently decided to contest the 2026 elections. The claim significantly overstated the certainty of the retirement.
article_title: Six Former PMs to Issue Joint Retirement Statement? Claim Exaggerated
named_entities: KP Sharma Oli, Sher Bahadur Deuba, Pushpa Kamal Dahal, Madhav Kumar Nepal, Jhalanath Khanal, Baburam Bhattarai

caption: KP Sharma Oli resignation letter blaming opposition to Lord Ram's birthplace in Ayodhya
full_claim: Indian news channels News18 and IBC24 claimed that former Prime Minister Oli had left behind a secret resignation letter blaming his ouster on opposition to Lord Ram's birthplace in Ayodhya.
true_context: No such letter existed. Khabarhub and BOOM verified that Oli's actual resignation letter cited 'extraordinary circumstances' and constitutional procedures — with no mention of Lord Ram or Ayodhya. The Indian channels fabricated the religious angle.
article_title: Indian Channels Claim Oli Left Secret Resignation Letter Blaming Lord Ram Controversy — False
named_entities: KP Sharma Oli, News18, IBC24, Lord Ram, Ayodhya, India

caption: Gen Z protesters attacking and vandalizing Pashupatinath temple in Kathmandu
full_claim: Indian TV channels NDTV and Times Now Navbharat shared footage claiming Gen Z protesters had attacked the Pashupatinath Temple during the September 2025 uprising.
true_context: BOOM fact-checked the video and found it was from March 2025, during the Basleshwar Jatra Festival — months before the Gen Z protests. No protesters attacked Pashupatinath. The footage was shared in a completely false context.
article_title: NDTV and Times Now Falsely Claim Gen Z Protesters Attacked Pashupatinath Temple
named_entities: NDTV, Times Now, Pashupatinath Temple, Gen Z Protest, Kathmandu

caption: Court acquits RSP chair Rabi Lamichhane in cooperative embezzlement case
full_claim: Viral posts and TikTok videos claimed RSP Chair Rabi Lamichhane had been acquitted by court of all charges related to cooperative embezzlement fraud.
true_context: No such acquittal occurred. Lamichhane remained in judicial custody at Nakkhu Prison and the cooperative fraud case was ongoing. Khabarhub confirmed directly through court records that no acquittal verdict had been issued.
article_title: Viral Claim That Rabi Lamichhane Was Acquitted in Cooperative Case Is False

caption: KP Sharma Oli secretly recommended Sushila Karki as prime minister before leaving Baluwatar
full_claim: Maoist Centre Chair Prachanda publicly claimed that he had read Oli's letter recommending Sushila Karki as Prime Minister after Oli left the official residence in Baluwatar on September 9.

Oli clarified he made only a general recommendation for the President to take steps within the constitutional framework. The specific claim about a Karki recommendation was disputed by Oli directly.

caption: Yogi Adityanath calling for restoration of monarchy at Hindu conference in Nepal 2023
full_claim: A viral video claimed to show Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath advocating for the return of monarchy at a Hindu conference in Nepal in 2023.
true_context: Nepal Check debunked the claim. The clip was from an entirely different event in Kathmandu in October 2016 — not a 2023 conference. Adityanath made no such statement at a 2023 Nepal event. The footage was recycled from 7 years prior.

full_claim: A post on X claimed protesters in Nepal's Gen Z uprising had carried Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath's photo during the demonstrations.
true_context: BOOM Live traced the image to a pro-monarchy rally in Kathmandu in March 2025 — six months before the Gen Z protests. The image had nothing to do with the Gen Z movement and was shared in completely the wrong context.

caption: Nepali protesters showing support for Indian PM Narendra Modi during Gen Z uprising
full_claim: A viral video circulated with captions claiming Nepalis were supporting Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi during the Gen Z protests.
true_context: Alt News fact-checked and confirmed the footage was from an Independence Day rally in Sikkim, India — not Nepal. The video had no connection to Nepal's Gen Z protest movement.

caption: KP Sharma Oli is the only Nepali prime minister who never visited India during his tenure
full_claim: Viral claims stated that KP Sharma Oli was the only Nepali prime minister who never visited India during his tenure in office.
true_context: Alt News fact-checked and confirmed the claim was false. Oli made official trips to India in 2016 and 2018 during his previous terms as prime minister. The claim ignored his documented visits.
article_title: Claim That KP Sharma Oli Was the Only Nepali PM Never to Visit India Is False

caption: Balen Shah and Dr. Sanduk Ruit secretly plotting political moves post Gen Z protests
full_claim: A viral video claimed to show a secret political meeting between Kathmandu Mayor Balen Shah and renowned eye surgeon Dr. Sanduk Ruit, suggesting political conspiracy between the two.
true_context: TechPana's analysis revealed the video was made by combining three separate clips from completely different times and contexts: a January 2023 congratulatory visit, a September 2025 press conference by Ruit about Hami Nepal, and a February 2023 interview with Sudan Gurung. There was no secret meeting.
article_title: Viral Video Falsely Claims Secret Political Meeting Between Balen Shah and Dr. Sanduk Ruit

caption: Prachanda admitting in speech that ballot boxes must be changed to win elections
full_claim: A viral video clip showed Prachanda appearing to say ballot boxes must be changed to win elections, with the claim circulating widely as an admission of election fraud plans.
true_context: TechPana's fact-check revealed the viral video was made by splicing together separate fragments of Prachanda's speeches from different contexts. His actual statement referred to party reform and electoral strategy — not an admission of ballot box tampering.

full_claim: Viral photos showed Sydney Opera House illuminated with Nepal's flag colors (red with moon and sun symbol), claiming this was done to honor Nepal.

full_claim: BBC Nepal and Nepal Police's official social media both claimed a viral video showing a dramatic cliff face was located in Chitwan, Nepal.
true_context: MySansar's investigation traced the actual location to Rukum district, not Chitwan — thousands of kilometers away. Both BBC Nepal and Nepal Police had published the wrong location information. The viral cliff was in western Nepal, not in Chitwan.

full_claim: Multiple news outlets including Ratopati and Lokantar reported that the Armed Police Force had seized two pickup trucks containing onions worth over Rs. 4.2 million.
true_context: NepalFactCheck/MySansar verified the onion seizure occurred but the value was significantly lower than Rs. 4.2 million. The actual market value of the seized onions was much less. The inflated price figure was reported without verification from APF or market sources.
article_title: सशस्त्र प्रहरीले ४२ लाखको प्याज नियन्त्रणमा लियो? खबर भ्रामक

full_claim: Viral Facebook posts claimed journalist Dilbhushan Pathak had been appointed as the chief editor of News 24 Television.

article_title: दिलभूषण पाठक न्यूज २४ को प्रधानसम्पादक नियुक्त? दाबी गलत

full_claim: A 51-second viral video clip purports to show Indian PM Narendra Modi speaking about his Akhand Bharat (Greater India) plan that would include Nepal's territory.

article_title: मोदीको 'अखण्ड भारत' को AI भिडियो — ५१ सेकेन्डको भाइरल झुट

caption: Sunita Dangol endorsing Home Minister Rabi Lamichhane's leadership
full_claim: A viral video claimed Kathmandu Deputy Mayor Sunita Dangol endorsed Deputy Prime Minister and Home Minister Rabi Lamichhane in a speech.
true_context: The video showed Dangol speaking about road safety during a traffic awareness event — not endorsing Lamichhane. Her speech was completely misrepresented to appear as political endorsement.
article_title: उपमेयर दाङ्गोलले उपप्रधानमन्त्री लामिछानेलाई समर्थन गरे? भ्रामक

true_context: The clip was from a UML party event where Oli spoke about party unity and defending against criticism — not about Balen Shah specifically. His general remarks about 'opponents' were mistakenly or deliberately reframed as targeting the mayor.
article_title: ओलीले बालेनलाई लक्षित गरेर बोलेको भिडियो? क्लिप सन्दर्भबाहिर

caption: AI-generated images of Jajarkot earthquake devastation circulating as real photos
full_claim: After the November 2023 Jajarkot earthquake, dramatic images of devastation were widely shared by influencers and organizations as real photographs from the disaster scene.
true_context: Reporters Nepal identified multiple widely-shared images as AI-generated. The images showed destruction more dramatic than the actual event, and reverse image search plus AI detection tools confirmed they were not real photographs from Jajarkot.
article_title: जाजरकोट भूकम्पका AI-निर्मित नक्कली तस्विरहरू सामाजिक सञ्जालमा भाइरल

caption: Sand dredgers crashing into bridge during Nepal floods 2024
full_claim: A viral video claimed to show sand dredgers crashing into a bridge during recent floods in Nepal's monsoon season.
true_context: The video was actually from Vietnam, showing the flooded Chay River following Typhoon Yagi in September 2024 — not Nepal. The dramatic footage was shared with Nepali captions during Nepal's own monsoon flood season.
article_title: नेपालमा बाढीले पुलमा ठोक्किएको भिडियो? खासमा भियतनामको थियो

full_claim: A viral video purported to show a wedding procession from Nepal's Goddess Sita's maternal home (Janakpur) to Ayodhya's Ram Temple in India.
true_context: The video was from a July 2023 ceremony in Greater Noida, India — not from Janakpur, Nepal. It had no connection to Sita's birthplace or the Ram Temple in Ayodhya.
article_title: देवी सीताको मातृगृहबाट अयोध्याको भिडियो? खासमा ग्रेटर नोइडाको थियो

true_context: Reporters Nepal traced the video back to a 2021 landslide in Japan. The dramatic footage was repurposed with Sikkim flood captions during the actual disaster to make it appear more devastating.

caption: Dr. Baburam Bhattarai hospitalized after heart attack
full_claim: Viral posts claimed Dr. Baburam Bhattarai had suffered a heart attack and been hospitalized.

caption: Madhav Kumar Nepal resigns from his party and announces retirement
full_claim: Viral posts claimed CPN-Unified Socialist Chairman Madhav Kumar Nepal had resigned from his party and announced his retirement from politics.

caption: Pro-monarchy rally for King Gyanendra funded by India and BJP
full_claim: Multiple posts claimed the 2025 pro-monarchy rallies supporting former King Gyanendra were funded by India's ruling BJP party to destabilize Nepal's republic.
true_context: No credible evidence of Indian funding for the rallies was presented in the viral posts. The claims appeared to be politically motivated. Reporters Nepal found no documentary proof, and the Indian Embassy denied involvement.

full_claim: Viral posts claimed Rabi Lamichhane had been acquitted by the court of all charges related to cooperative embezzlement.
true_context: No such acquittal occurred. At the time of the viral claim, the case was ongoing. Lamichhane remained in judicial custody and the cooperative case continued through the courts.

full_claim: A video circulated showing Sushila Karki taking an oath was shared as showing her swearing in as Prime Minister of Nepal.
true_context: The video was old — it showed Karki being sworn in as Chief Justice or to a previous constitutional position, not as Prime Minister. The clip was reused when she was later appointed PM to falsely suggest this was her PM swearing-in ceremony.

full_claim: Prime Minister Sher Bahadur Deuba claimed his government built more roads in Kathmandu Valley than any previous administration in the same time period.

true_context: World Bank and Central Bureau of Statistics data showed Nepal's GDP growth was approximately 7.5% for that fiscal year — real but slightly overstated. The 'highest in South Asia' claim was also inaccurate as Bangladesh and India recorded higher growth.

full_claim: The Nepal government declared Nepal fully Open Defecation Free (ODF), meaning 100% of citizens have access to sanitation facilities.
true_context: Multiple surveys including NDHS data showed that access to proper sanitation was not universal, particularly in remote hill and mountain districts. The 100% ODF claim was premature and not supported by independent field verification.
article_title: Is Nepal Really Open Defecation Free? Government Claim Checked
named_entities: Nepal Government, Ministry of Water Supply, NDHS

full_claim: Multiple Nepali politicians and social media users claimed India's new political map released in November 2019 included Nepal's Kalapani, Lipulekh, and Limpiyadhura territories.
true_context: This claim was verified as TRUE. India's new political map did show Kalapani, Lipulekh, and Limpiyadhura as part of Indian territory, which Nepal formally protested through its Foreign Ministry.
article_title: Claims About Kalapani Territory in India's New Map: Checked
named_entities: India, Nepal, Kalapani, Lipulekh, Limpiyadhura, Ministry of Foreign Affairs

caption: สล็อตเว็บตรง PG SLOT เกมสล็อต เล่นง่าย ภาพสวยที่สุดในไทย
full_claim: Multiple politicians and social media posts claimed the US Millennium Challenge Corporation (MCC) compact with Nepal would override Nepal's constitution, laws, and sovereignty.
true_context: Legal experts and the MCC agreement itself showed the compact does not supersede Nepal's constitution. The agreement contains standard language about dispute resolution used in all MCC compacts. Claims of sovereignty threats were exaggerated and politically motivated.
article_title: Does MCC Compact Threaten Nepal's Sovereignty? Claims Checked
named_entities: MCC, United States, Nepal Government, Pushpa Kamal Dahal, KP Sharma Oli

caption: สล็อตเว็บตรง PG SLOT เกมสล็อต เล่นง่าย ภาพสวยที่สุดในไทย
full_claim: The Nepal government claimed the national poverty rate had been reduced to 18% due to its development programs and remittance economy.
true_context: While CBS Nepal data showed poverty reduction, the 18% figure was disputed by World Bank estimates which placed it higher. Additionally, COVID-19 had reversed many gains, and the claim did not reflect post-pandemic conditions.
article_title: Government's Claim of Reducing Nepal's Poverty Rate: Checked
named_entities: Nepal Government, Central Bureau of Statistics, World Bank

caption: สล็อตเว็บตรง PG SLOT เกมสล็อต เล่นง่าย ภาพสวยที่สุดในไทย
full_claim: Social media posts and opposition politicians claimed Nepal's government was significantly undercounting COVID-19 deaths, with actual deaths being three to five times the official figures.
true_context: Excess mortality data analysis by health researchers suggested death undercounting was likely due to limited testing capacity in rural areas. However, the 'three to five times' figure was not independently verified. The Ministry of Health acknowledged data collection challenges.
article_title: Is Nepal Government Undercounting COVID-19 Deaths? Claims Checked
named_entities: Nepal Ministry of Health, COVID-19, Nepal Government

caption: สล็อตเว็บตรง PG SLOT เกมสล็อต เล่นง่าย ภาพสวยที่สุดในไทย
full_claim: Finance Minister claimed the 2020/21 national budget allocated the highest-ever percentage of GDP to education in Nepal's history.
true_context: Analysis of budget documents showed education's share of GDP was 4.2%, slightly below the 4.5% allocated in 2017/18. The 'highest ever' claim was incorrect based on Ministry of Finance data.
article_title: Finance Minister's Claim About Education Budget Allocation: Checked
named_entities: Nepal Finance Minister, Ministry of Finance, Ministry of Education

caption: สล็อตเว็บตรง PG SLOT เกมสล็อต เล่นง่าย ภาพสวยที่สุดในไทย
full_claim: PM Sher Bahadur Deuba claimed tourism revenue doubled under his government compared to the previous administration.
true_context: Ministry of Tourism data showed tourism revenue grew approximately 22% — not doubled. While Visit Nepal Year preparations boosted infrastructure spending, actual revenue growth was well below the '100% increase' claimed.
article_title: PM Deuba's Claim on Tourism Revenue Growth: Fact Check
named_entities: Sher Bahadur Deuba, Nepali Congress, Ministry of Tourism

caption: สล็อตเว็บตรง PG SLOT เกมสล็อต เล่นง่าย ภาพสวยที่สุดในไทย
full_claim: A circulating claim stated Nepal spends more on its defence/army than on health, making it one of the most militarized countries per capita in South Asia.
true_context: Budget data showed health received a significantly larger allocation than defence. The claim was false — Nepal's defence spending as a share of GDP is among the lowest in South Asia.
article_title: Claim That Nepal Spends More on Defence Than Health: Checked
named_entities: Nepal Army, Nepal Ministry of Finance, Ministry of Health

caption: สล็อตเว็บตรง PG SLOT เกมสล็อต เล่นง่าย ภาพสวยที่สุดในไทย
full_claim: Social media posts claimed over 10 million Nepalis had signed a petition demanding the restoration of Gyanendra Shah as king of Nepal.
true_context: The online petition existed but had far fewer signatures — under 500,000 at the time of fact-checking. Many signatures appeared to be duplicates or from non-Nepali IP addresses. The '10 million' figure was fabricated.
article_title: Claim That Millions Signed Petition to Restore Gyanendra as King: Checked
named_entities: Gyanendra Shah, Rastriya Prajatantra Party, Nepal

caption: สล็อตเว็บตรง PG SLOT เกมสล็อต เล่นง่าย ภาพสวยที่สุดในไทย
full_claim: Politicians and media reports claimed Nepal's federal parliament has achieved 33% female representation, ranking among the highest in Asia.
true_context: This was verified as broadly accurate. After the 2017 elections, women held approximately 32.7% of seats in the Federal Parliament — close to the constitutional requirement of 33%, and one of the highest rates in Asia.
article_title: Claim That Nepal Has 33% Women in Parliament: Checked
named_entities: Nepal Federal Parliament, Election Commission Nepal

caption: สล็อตเว็บตรง PG SLOT เกมสล็อต เล่นง่าย ภาพสวยที่สุดในไทย
full_claim: CPN-UML leader Bhim Rawal claimed Nepal's economy had become 'completely dependent' on remittances and that without remittances the country would collapse within months.
true_context: While remittances constitute a significant share of Nepal's GDP (approximately 25-27%), the claim of 'complete dependence' was an exaggeration. Agriculture, tourism, and services also contribute substantially. The economy would face severe strain, not immediate collapse, without remittances.
article_title: Bhim Rawal's Claim That Remittances Are Nepal's Only Economy: Checked
named_entities: Bhim Rawal, CPN-UML, Nepal Rastra Bank

caption: สล็อตเว็บตรง PG SLOT เกมสล็อต เล่นง่าย ภาพสวยที่สุดในไทย
full_claim: Opposition politicians and social media claimed the government had secretly sold all of Nepal's future hydropower production to India at extremely cheap rates, against the national interest.
true_context: Nepal had signed Power Trade Agreements with India for specific projects, not 'all' hydropower. Rates were negotiated and reviewed by the Nepal Electricity Authority. The 'all hydropower sold secretly' claim was an exaggeration of actual agreements.
article_title: Claim That Nepal Sold All Its Hydropower to India at Cheap Rates: Checked
named_entities: Nepal Electricity Authority, India, Nepal Government, Power Trade Agreement

caption: สล็อตเว็บตรง PG SLOT เกมสล็อต เล่นง่าย ภาพสวยที่สุดในไทย
full_claim: Social media posts and opposition parties claimed House Speaker Agni Sapkota was directly responsible for the disappearance of Arjun Lama during the Maoist insurgency and should be prosecuted.
true_context: A Nepal court had issued an arrest warrant for Sapkota in 2010 in connection with the case. The Supreme Court later stayed the warrant. The factual basis of the case (a warrant existed) was confirmed, though Sapkota denied involvement. The issue remained in the courts.
article_title: Claims About House Speaker Agni Sapkota and War Crimes: Checked
named_entities: Agni Sapkota, Arjun Lama, CPN Maoist Centre, Nepal Supreme Court

caption: สล็อตเว็บตรง PG SLOT เกมสล็อต เล่นง่าย ภาพสวยที่สุดในไทย
full_claim: CPN-Unified Socialist leader Madhav Kumar Nepal claimed the water crisis in Kathmandu Valley had dramatically worsened under the current government, with supply falling to record lows.
true_context: KUKL data showed water supply in Kathmandu Valley remained broadly consistent with previous years. While shortages existed, particularly in summer, the claim of 'dramatically worsened' and 'record lows' under the specific government was not supported by supply data.
article_title: Madhav Kumar Nepal's Claim About Water Crisis in Kathmandu: Checked
named_entities: Madhav Kumar Nepal, CPN-Unified Socialist, KUKL, Kathmandu Valley

caption: สล็อตเว็บตรง PG SLOT เกมสล็อต เล่นง่าย ภาพสวยที่สุดในไทย
full_claim: NC leader Gagan Thapa claimed Nepal had fallen to become 'one of the most corrupt countries in Asia' according to international rankings.
true_context: Transparency International's 2021 Corruption Perceptions Index showed Nepal at 117/180, mid-range globally. While corruption remains a concern, the 'most corrupt in Asia' claim was inaccurate — several other Asian countries ranked lower than Nepal.
article_title: Gagan Thapa's Claim About Nepal's Corruption Ranking: Checked
named_entities: Gagan Thapa, Nepali Congress, Transparency International

caption: สล็อตเว็บตรง PG SLOT เกมสล็อต เล่นง่าย ภาพสวยที่สุดในไทย
full_claim: Social media users shared dramatic photos of wildfires to highlight Nepal's 2021 forest fire season, claiming the photos showed fires in Nepal's forests.
true_context: South Asia Check verified multiple viral images were actually from wildfires in the US, Australia, and Brazil — not Nepal. While Nepal did experience real wildfires that season, the most dramatic viral photos were from other countries.
article_title: Viral Wildfire Photos From Foreign Countries Shared as Nepal Fires: Checked

caption: สล็อตเว็บตรง PG SLOT เกมสล็อต เล่นง่าย ภาพสวยที่สุดในไทย
full_claim: Dr. Baburam Bhattarai claimed the 2015 constitution of Nepal fully guaranteed Madhesi community rights and that demands for amendment were politically motivated.
true_context: Constitutional law experts and Madhesi community leaders noted several provisions remained contested, including electoral constituency delimitation and proportional representation formulas. The 'fully guaranteed' claim was disputed by affected communities and later led to formal constitutional amendments in 2016.
article_title: Baburam Bhattarai's Claim About Madhesi Rights in Constitution: Checked

caption: A photo viral on social media claims to be a recent one from India. The photo shows a woman riding a bicycle with an infant wrapped on her back and a sack tugged on to the cycles rear seat. This image circulated widely, falsely implying it was a current visual from India during the COVID lockdown.
full_claim: A photo viral on social media claims to be a recent one from India. The photo shows a woman riding a bicycle with an infant wrapped on her back and a sack tugged on to the cycles rear seat. This image circulated widely, falsely implying it was a current visual from India during the COVID lockdown.
true_context: The photo was originally captured by photographer Narendra Sreshtha on June 29, 2012, in Nepal. Its original caption clearly states it depicts a Nepalese mother carrying a child on her back as she travels on a bicycle towards Nepalgunj city, Nepal. Therefore, this image is unequivocally from Nepal, not India, and is an old photograph from 2012, completely unrelated to any recent COVID lockdown events in India. Its viral circulation as a recent Indian scene is a clear instance of outofcontext misinformation.
